response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with a broad polyglot background, but their skillset is fundamentally misaligned with this role. C++ is not among their listed skills and substantive C++ work is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project — neither of which demonstrates the depth, architectural ownership, or concurrent/networked systems expertise the role demands. There is no evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443 or any industrial cybersecurity standards, MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, or related protocols. The candidate's strengths lie in TypeScript/Node.js web services and event-driven web platforms, which do not translate directly to the on-premises C++ platform engineering this position requires.
strengths: Genuine event-driven architecture experience through the GISCollective geo-data platform and high-throughput microservices work at Sociomantic.
Strong software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and a track record of mentoring through workshops and Selenium courses.
React and Node.js exposure satisfies some of the listed advantageous skills, and D-language async/fiber work shows low-latency systems awareness.
concerns: C++ is absent from the skills section and has no meaningful recent usage; role requires 8–12 years of C++/STL depth as a core competency.
No industrial automation, IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards, or relevant protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, DNP 3.0) anywhere in the resume.
Primary expertise is web and full-stack JavaScript/TypeScript — a fundamentally different domain from the secure, high-performance on-premises C++ platform engineering this role targets.
